questions.question: Which theorem states that if a function is continuous on a closed interval [a, b] and differentiable on the open interval (a, b), there exists at least one c in (a, b) where the instantaneous rate of change equals the average rate of change?
What is the value of the limit as x approaches 0 of (sin(x) / x)?
What technique evaluates an integral by switching coordinates using a matrix of partial derivatives called the Jacobian?
The derivative of which function is equal to sec^2(x)?
What type of series alternates signs and converges if its terms strictly decrease in absolute value to 0?
Which multivariable calculus operation calculates the scalar 'flux density' of a vector field leaving an infinitesimal volume?
What mathematical constant is defined as the limit of (1 + 1/n)^n as n approaches infinity?
If a function's second derivative is strictly positive on an interval, what geometric property does the graph of the function possess on that interval?
What improper integral evaluates to the square root of pi?
answers: The integral from -inf to inf of e^(-x^2) dx
The integral from 0 to inf of 1/x dx
The integral from -1 to 1 of sqrt(1-x^2) dx
The integral from 0 to pi of sin(x) dx
questions.correctAnswer: The integral from -inf to inf of e^(-x^2) dx

questions.question: Which branch of mathematical analysis rigorously treats concepts of lengths, areas, and volumes via generalized functions?
